questions.question: Consider a binary search tree with the following structure:     4     / \    2   6       / \      1       5 Write a function to insert a node with value 3 into this tree. Represent your answer in the form of a binary tree, where 0 represents a null node.
questions.hint: Use the standard representation of binary trees where 0 represents a null node.

questions.question: Design an algorithm to find the maximum value in a binary search tree. Use a step-by-step approach to explain your solution.
questions.hint: Use the properties of binary search trees to guide your solution.

questions.question: Write a function to search for a node with value 5 in the following binary search tree:     4     / \    2   6       / \      1       5        \/\       \      \   3       \   7        \    \        \   8        \    \        \   9        \       \        \     \       \        \  10       \        \       \       \        \       \     \       \        \     \        \   \        \        \     \        \   \     \       \       \       \    \        \       \    \        \        \       \       \     \        \       \       \        \       \        \       \       \     \        \        \        \        \        \     \        \    \
questions.hint: Use a recursive or iterative approach to search for the node.
